skill.name: zinc-database
skill.description: Access the ZINC (230M+ purchasable compounds) database when you need to look up compounds by ZINC ID/SMILES, run similarity/analog searches, or download 3D ready-to-dock structures for virtual screening and drug discovery.
skill.summary: Query the ZINC database of 230M+ purchasable compounds for drug discovery and virtual screening workflows.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: This skill provides legitimate access to the ZINC public chemical database (docking.org) for drug discovery research. The static scanner flagged 296 potential issues, but the vast majority are false positives from documentation examples (curl commands in markdown code blocks, references to scientific algorithms, and legitimate public database URLs). The primary real risk is subprocess.run usage at SKILL.md:52, which invokes curl with hardcoded arguments to query the ZINC API. The heuristic dangerous combination finding (code execution + network + filesystem) is the tool's intended functionality for a scientific research workflow. All prior audit vetogates passed. Risk level is medium due to external command execution, not due to malicious intent.

high_findings.title: External Command Execution via subprocess.run
high_findings.description: Python subprocess.run calls curl with hardcoded URL arguments. While the URLs point to a legitimate public research database (cartblanche22.docking.org), executing external commands from within an AI agent introduces a real attack surface if the URL is modified or redirected.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Examples in Documentation
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md contains many shell command examples using backtick execution syntax and curl commands embedded in markdown code blocks. These are documentation examples for user reference, not executed code, but may confuse automated processing tools.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded External URLs in Documentation
low_findings.description: The skill documentation contains hardcoded URLs to legitimate public research databases (docking.org, zinc.docking.org). These are intentional references to the ZINC database API and documentation sites. No malicious destinations detected.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Patterns in API Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The documentation shows URL path patterns like `.../substances.txt:zinc_id=<ID>&output_fields=...` which are legitimate API endpoint examples for the CartBlanche22 service, not actual path traversal attacks.

dangerous_patterns.title: subprocess.run with External URL
dangerous_patterns.description: subprocess.run is called with a URL constructed from a hardcoded base URL and user-provided query parameters. The base URL is a legitimate research database API, and no shell injection vector exists since arguments are passed as a list. However, this pattern should be monitored for any future changes that might introduce untrusted URL components.

content.user_title: Search and retrieve compounds from the ZINC database
content.value_statement: Drug discovery researchers need fast access to the 230 million purchasable compounds in the ZINC database. This skill lets you query compounds by ZINC ID or SMILES structure, run similarity searches, and download 3D docking-ready structures without manual web browsing.
actual_capabilities: Look up compounds by ZINC ID in single or batch mode
Search chemical structures by SMILES with configurable similarity thresholds
Retrieve random compound samples from purchasable subsets
Parse ZINC tranche codes to filter compounds by H-bond donors, LogP, molecular weight, and reactivity
Query supplier and catalog identifiers for vendor information
Download 3D structures in MOL2, SDF, and DB2 formats organized by tranche
limitations: Requires curl and Python 3.9+ to be installed in the execution environment
Query performance depends on the CartBlanche22 API server availability and load
Similarity search parameters (dist, adist) require domain knowledge to use effectively
Large batch downloads may need additional tooling like wget or aria2c for efficiency

use_cases.title: Virtual screening library construction
use_cases.description: A computational chemist samples purchasable compounds from the ZINC database using random sampling and tranche property filters to build a focused screening library for a drug target.
use_cases.target_user: Computational chemists and drug discovery researchers

use_cases.title: Compound lookup and validation
use_cases.description: A medicinal chemist retrieves compound details by ZINC ID or SMILES string to verify structures, check supplier availability, and review physicochemical properties before ordering.

use_cases.title: Docking pipeline preparation
use_cases.description: A structural biologist downloads 3D docking-ready structures organized by ZINC tranche for use in DOCK or AutoDock Vina virtual screening pipelines.

prompt_templates.title: Look up a compound by ZINC ID
prompt_templates.prompt: Look up compound ZINC000000000001 in the ZINC database. Show me the SMILES, supplier catalogs, and tranche properties.
prompt_templates.scenario: Retrieving compound details using a known ZINC identifier

prompt_templates.title: Search for similar compounds by structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the ZINC database for compounds similar to c1ccccc1 (benzene) with a distance of 3. Show me the top 10 results with their SMILES and tranche codes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Finding structural analogs of a known compound

prompt_templates.title: Sample compounds for screening
prompt_templates.prompt: Sample 100 random lead-like compounds from the ZINC database. Parse the tranche codes and filter for compounds with molecular weight under 400 and LogP under 4.0.
prompt_templates.scenario: Building a focused screening library with property filters

prompt_templates.title: Download 3D structures for docking
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the download URL for 3D structures of compounds in the H05P035M400-0 tranche from the ZINC22 files library. List the available file formats.
prompt_templates.scenario: Retrieving docking-ready 3D structures for virtual screening

output_examples.input: Search ZINC database for compounds similar to CC(C)O (isopropyl alcohol) with dist=2
output: Found 47 compounds similar to CC(C)O (isopropyl alcohol) with distance <= 2.

output_examples.input: Show me 5 random fragment-like compounds from ZINC with their properties
output: 5 random fragment-like compounds from ZINC database:
ZINC000022345678 - Tranche H02P025M200-0 - 2 H-bond donors, LogP 2.5, MW 200 Da
ZINC000045678901 - Tranche H01P018M160-0 - 1 H-bond donor, LogP 1.8, MW 160 Da
ZINC000078901234 - Tranche H03P020M250-0 - 3 H-bond donors, LogP 2.0, MW 250 Da
ZINC000101234567 - Tranche H01P030M180-0 - 1 H-bond donor, LogP 3.0, MW 180 Da
ZINC000123456789 - Tranche H02P015M220-0 - 2 H-bond donors, LogP 1.5, MW 220 Da

best_practices: Request only the output fields you need to reduce API response size and improve query speed
Start with exact SMILES matches (dist=0) before expanding to broader similarity searches
Use tranche code parsing to filter compounds by physicochemical properties server-side before downloading 3D structures
anti_patterns: Do not query the ZINC API with extremely large batch requests in a single call. Split large batches into smaller groups.
Do not ignore the dist and adist parameters. Using default values may miss relevant analogs or return too many results.
Do not download full tranche archives without first sampling and validating compounds of interest to save time and bandwidth.

faq.question: What is the ZINC database?
faq.answer: ZINC is a free public database of over 230 million commercially available compounds for virtual screening and drug discovery.

faq.question: What software dependencies does this skill need?
faq.answer: The skill needs curl (7.70+) and Python 3.9+ with pandas. Optional dependencies include requests and RDKit for structure validation.

faq.question: What are ZINC tranche codes?
faq.answer: Tranche codes encode compound properties in the format H##P###M###-phase. They represent H-bond donors, LogP multiplied by 10, molecular weight in Daltons, and reactivity phase.

faq.question: Can I search for compounds by structure?
faq.answer: Yes. You can search by SMILES string with optional dist and adist parameters to control the similarity threshold for analog expansion.

faq.question: How do I download 3D structures for docking?
faq.answer: Use the ZINC22 files library at files.docking.org/zinc22. Files are organized by tranche and available in MOL2, SDF, and DB2.GZ formats.

faq.question: Is the ZINC API free to use?
faq.answer: Yes. The CartBlanche22 API and ZINC database are free public resources for academic and research use.
